name: Adam-ondi-Ahman
description: A place in Daviess County, Missouri identified as where Adam blessed his posterity. Named by the Lord; also called Spring Hill.
significance: Where Adam gathered his posterity; prophesied location of a future gathering before Christ's return

name: Ammonihah
description: A wicked Nephite city where Alma and Amulek preached. The inhabitants rejected the gospel and were later completely destroyed by a Lamanite army.
significance: Site of Alma and Amulek's mission; believers were burned; later destroyed and called Desolation of Nehors

name: Antioch (Syria)
description: Third largest city in the Roman Empire. Believers were first called Christians here. Base for Paul's missionary journeys. Major early church center.
significance: Where believers were first called Christians; Paul's mission base

name: Babylon
description: Capital of the Babylonian Empire. Site of the Tower of Babel. Nebuchadnezzar destroyed Jerusalem and exiled Judah here. Daniel and Ezekiel prophesied during the exile.
significance: Site of the Exile; symbol of worldly power and opposition to God

name: Bethlehem
description: Birthplace of Jesus Christ and King David. Rachel was buried nearby. Ruth and Boaz's story takes place here.
significance: Birthplace of David and Jesus; fulfillment of Micah's prophecy

name: Bountiful
description: A prosperous Nephite land and city in the New World, located near the narrow neck of land. Also the name of the coastal land where Lehi's family built their ship in the Old World.
significance: Where Christ appeared to the Nephites after his resurrection; strategic military location near the narrow neck of land

name: Canaan
description: The land promised by God to Abraham and his descendants. The Israelites conquered it under Joshua after the Exodus from Egypt.
significance: The Promised Land given by covenant to Abraham's descendants

name: Capernaum
description: Fishing village on the north shore of the Sea of Galilee that served as Jesus's home base during his Galilean ministry. Site of many healings and teachings.
significance: Jesus's ministry headquarters in Galilee

name: Cumorah
description: A hill and surrounding land of many waters, rivers, and fountains where the final Nephite battle took place. Called Ramah by the Jaredites.
significance: Site of the final destruction of the Nephite nation; where Mormon hid the sacred records

name: Damascus
description: Ancient city in Syria. Paul was traveling to Damascus to persecute Christians when he saw a vision of Christ and was converted. Ananias baptized him here.
significance: Site of Paul's conversion; one of the world's oldest cities

name: Dead Sea
description: Hypersaline lake at the lowest point on earth, bordering ancient Israel and Moab. The cities of Sodom and Gomorrah were traditionally located near its shores.
significance: Near Sodom and Gomorrah; boundary marker of the Promised Land

name: Eden
description: The garden planted by God eastward in Eden where Adam and Eve were placed. A river went out of Eden to water the garden.
significance: Where God placed Adam and Eve; site of the Fall of man

name: Egypt
description: Ancient civilization along the Nile where Israel was enslaved for centuries. The Exodus from Egypt is the defining event of Israelite identity. The Holy Family fled here after Jesus's birth.
significance: Site of Israel's bondage and the Exodus; refuge for the Holy Family

name: Egypt
description: The ancient kingdom south of Canaan where Abraham sojourned during a famine. First discovered and settled by Egyptus, daughter of Ham.
significance: Where Abraham sojourned; origin of Pharaoh's dynasty from Ham's lineage

name: Euphrates River
description: One of the four rivers of Eden and the northeastern boundary of the Promised Land. Major river of Mesopotamia running through Babylon.
significance: Boundary of the Promised Land; river of Eden and Babylon

name: Galilee
description: Northern region of Israel, home district of Jesus and most of his apostles. Jesus performed the majority of his miracles and teachings in this region.
significance: Primary region of Jesus's ministry

name: Garden of Eden
description: Paradise garden planted by God where Adam and Eve lived before the Fall. Located near the Tigris and Euphrates rivers according to Genesis.
significance: Original dwelling of humanity; setting of the Fall

name: Gethsemane
description: Garden at the foot of the Mount of Olives where Jesus prayed in agony and was arrested the night before his crucifixion.
significance: Site of Jesus's agonizing prayer and betrayal by Judas

name: Golgotha
description: The place outside Jerusalem where Jesus was crucified. The name means 'Place of the Skull' in Aramaic.
significance: Site of Jesus's crucifixion

name: Hebron
description: One of the oldest cities in the world. Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Sarah, Rebekah, and Leah are buried in the Cave of Machpelah here. David's first capital.
significance: Burial place of the patriarchs; David's first capital

name: Independence
description: A city in Missouri designated by revelation as the 'center place' of Zion, with a spot identified for a future temple.
significance: Designated as the center place of Zion and location for a future temple

name: Jericho
description: First Canaanite city conquered by Israel when its walls fell after seven days of marching. In the NT, Jesus healed blind Bartimaeus and called Zacchaeus here.
significance: First city conquered in the Promised Land; site of NT miracles

name: Jerusalem
description: Capital of ancient Israel and Judah, site of Solomon's Temple and the Second Temple. Central to the ministry, crucifixion, and resurrection of Jesus Christ.
significance: Most important city in scripture; spiritual center of Judaism and Christianity

name: Jordan River
description: Major river flowing from the Sea of Galilee to the Dead Sea. Israel crossed it to enter the Promised Land. Naaman was healed in its waters. Jesus was baptized here by John.
significance: Boundary of the Promised Land; site of Jesus's baptism

name: Judea
description: Southern region centered on Jerusalem, territory of the tribe of Judah. The southern kingdom after the split. In NT times, the Roman province containing Jerusalem.
significance: Territory of Judah; location of Jerusalem and the Temple

name: Kirtland
description: A city in northeastern Ohio where the early Latter-day Saints gathered, built a temple, and received many revelations.
significance: Site of the Kirtland Temple; center of Church operations; where many D&C sections were received

name: Mormon
description: A place with waters and a forest where Alma the Elder baptized believers and established the Church. Located in the borders of the land of Nephi.
significance: Where Alma performed the first recorded Nephite baptisms and organized the church in secret

name: Mount Carmel
description: Mountain range in northern Israel where Elijah challenged and defeated the 450 prophets of Baal, proving the Lord is God.
significance: Elijah's contest with the prophets of Baal

name: Mount Moriah
description: Mountain where Abraham was commanded to sacrifice Isaac. Later identified as the site where Solomon built the Temple in Jerusalem.
significance: Site of Abraham's near-sacrifice of Isaac and Solomon's Temple

name: Mount Sinai
description: Mountain where God gave Moses the Ten Commandments and the Law. Also where Elijah fled and encountered God in a still small voice.
significance: Where God delivered the Law to Moses; covenant foundation of Israel

name: Mount of Olives
description: Mountain east of Jerusalem where Jesus often taught his disciples, delivered the Olivet Discourse, and ascended into heaven. David fled over it during Absalom's rebellion.
significance: Site of Jesus's ascension and the Olivet Discourse

name: Nauvoo
description: A city in Illinois on the Mississippi River where the Saints gathered after being expelled from Missouri. Site of the Nauvoo Temple and the Nauvoo House.
significance: Major gathering place of the Saints; site of the Nauvoo Temple; where Joseph Smith was martyred nearby

name: Nazareth
description: Hometown of Jesus where he grew up. The angel Gabriel announced the birth of Jesus to Mary here. Jesus was rejected by his hometown synagogue.
significance: Hometown of Jesus; site of the Annunciation

name: Nephi
description: A major land and city in the southern portion of Book of Mormon geography. Originally settled by Nephi after separating from his brothers, later occupied by Lamanites.
significance: Original Nephite settlement; later the Lamanite capital; site of King Noah's reign and Abinadi's preaching

name: Nile River
description: Great river of Egypt, turned to blood as the first plague. Baby Moses was placed in a basket on the Nile and found by Pharaoh's daughter.
significance: Site of Moses's rescue and the first plague

name: Nineveh
description: Capital of the Assyrian Empire. God sent Jonah to preach repentance here, and the city repented. Later prophets Nahum and Zephaniah prophesied its destruction.
significance: City that repented at Jonah's preaching; capital of Assyria

name: Red Sea
description: Body of water miraculously parted by God through Moses, allowing the Israelites to escape Egypt while Pharaoh's army was drowned.
significance: Site of the miraculous parting during the Exodus

name: Rome
description: Capital of the Roman Empire. Paul wrote his epistle to the Romans and was eventually imprisoned and martyred here. Center of the early Gentile church.
significance: Capital of the empire; destination of Paul's ministry

name: Samaria
description: Capital of the northern kingdom of Israel built by King Omri. Fell to Assyria in 722 BC. In the NT era, Jesus ministered to the Samaritan woman at a nearby well.
significance: Capital of the northern kingdom; center of Samaritan worship

name: Sea of Galilee
description: Freshwater lake in northern Israel where Jesus called his first disciples, walked on water, calmed the storm, and performed many miracles along its shores.
significance: Center of Jesus's Galilean ministry; site of many miracles

name: Sidon
description: The most prominent river in the Book of Mormon, flowing past Zarahemla. Site of numerous battles and baptisms.
significance: Major geographical landmark; bodies of slain soldiers were cast into it during multiple wars

name: Zarahemla
description: Capital city of the Nephite nation and center of government for much of the Book of Mormon narrative. Destroyed by fire at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Political and spiritual capital of the Nephites; site of many sermons, wars, and the seat of the chief judges

name: Zion (City of Enoch)
description: A city built by Enoch whose people were of one heart and one mind. The entire city was taken up into heaven.
significance: The city translated to heaven because of its righteousness; model for future Zion

name: Antionum
description: The land of the Zoramites, east of Zarahemla and bordering the seashore, south of Jershon.
significance: Where Alma preached to the apostate Zoramites and their Rameumptom

name: Assyria
description: Powerful Mesopotamian empire that conquered the northern kingdom of Israel in 722 BC, scattering the ten tribes.
significance: Conquered northern Israel; scattered the ten tribes

name: Athens
description: Cultural capital of the ancient world where Paul preached on Mars Hill (the Areopagus), delivering his famous sermon about the 'Unknown God.'
significance: Paul's Mars Hill sermon on the Unknown God

name: Beersheba
description: Southernmost major city of Israel. Abraham and Abimelech made a covenant here. Isaac built an altar here. 'From Dan to Beersheba' described the full extent of Israel.
significance: Southern boundary of Israel; patriarchal covenant site

name: Bethany
description: Village near Jerusalem, home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus. Jesus raised Lazarus from the dead here and was anointed with perfume before his final Passover.
significance: Home of Lazarus; site of the resurrection miracle

name: Bethel
description: Place where Jacob dreamed of a ladder reaching to heaven with angels ascending and descending. A major worship center in ancient Israel, later condemned for its golden calf.
significance: Jacob's ladder vision; later center of unauthorized worship

name: Bethsaida
description: Fishing village on the Sea of Galilee, hometown of Peter, Andrew, and Philip. Jesus healed a blind man here and fed the 5,000 nearby.
significance: Home of Peter, Andrew, Philip; feeding of the 5,000 nearby

name: Bountiful (Old World)
description: A fertile coastal land where Lehi's family arrived after traveling through the Arabian wilderness. They built their ship here before crossing the ocean.
significance: Where Nephi built the ship to cross the ocean to the promised land

name: Caesarea
description: Roman administrative capital of Judea built by Herod the Great. Peter baptized Cornelius here. Paul was imprisoned here for two years before sailing to Rome.
significance: Roman capital of Judea; site of Cornelius's conversion and Paul's imprisonment

name: Caesarea Philippi
description: City at the base of Mount Hermon where Peter confessed Jesus as the Christ and Jesus declared he would build his church on that rock.
significance: Peter's confession of Christ; 'upon this rock I will build my church'

name: Cana
description: Village in Galilee where Jesus performed his first miracle, turning water into wine at a wedding feast.
significance: Jesus's first miracle — water to wine

name: Cave of Machpelah
description: Cave and field near Hebron that Abraham purchased as a burial site. Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, Rebekah, Jacob, and Leah were buried here.
significance: Burial place of the patriarchs and matriarchs

name: Chaldea
description: The region in southern Mesopotamia where Abraham grew up among idolatrous peoples who practiced human sacrifice.
significance: Land of Abraham's birth and early life; land of famine and idolatry from which he was called out

name: Corinth
description: Major Greek commercial city where Paul established a church and spent 18 months teaching. He wrote two epistles to the Corinthians addressing church issues.
significance: Major Pauline church; recipient of two epistles

name: Cyprus
description: Large Mediterranean island and homeland of Barnabas. Paul and Barnabas began their first missionary journey here.
significance: Barnabas's homeland; first missionary journey started here

name: Dan
description: Northernmost city of Israel, originally called Laish. Jeroboam set up a golden calf here. 'From Dan to Beersheba' described the full extent of Israel.
significance: Northern boundary of Israel; site of Jeroboam's golden calf

name: Desolation
description: A land and city in the northward region, named by the Nephites because of the destroyed Jaredite civilization found there.
significance: Named for the ruins of the Jaredites; site of battles in the final wars of the Nephites

name: Emmaus
description: Village about seven miles from Jerusalem where the resurrected Jesus appeared to two disciples on the road, though they did not recognize him until he broke bread.
significance: Resurrected Christ appeared to two disciples on the road

name: Ephesus
description: Major city in Asia Minor with a famous temple of Artemis. Paul ministered here for three years. One of the seven churches addressed in Revelation.
significance: Paul's longest ministry stay; one of the seven churches of Revelation

name: Far West
description: A city in Caldwell County, Missouri designated as a holy and consecrated land. Saints were commanded to build a temple there.
significance: Designated as a holy land; temple site commanded; headquarters before the Missouri expulsion

name: Fayette
description: A town in Seneca County, New York where the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints was formally organized on April 6, 1830.
significance: Birthplace of the restored Church; site of organizational meeting at Peter Whitmer farm

name: Galatia
description: Region in central Asia Minor where Paul established churches. He wrote the Epistle to the Galatians defending justification by faith.
significance: Recipient of Paul's epistle on justification by faith

name: Gath
description: One of the five major Philistine cities, home of Goliath the giant whom David defeated. David later fled to Gath and feigned madness before King Achish.
significance: Home of Goliath; David's refuge

name: Gaza
description: One of the five major Philistine cities. Samson carried away its gates and was later imprisoned and died here pulling down the temple of Dagon. Philip baptized the Ethiopian eunuch on the road to Gaza.
significance: Philistine city; Samson's death; Ethiopian eunuch's baptism route

name: Gibeon
description: Canaanite city whose inhabitants tricked Joshua into making a peace treaty. God made the sun stand still here during Joshua's battle. Solomon received wisdom in a dream here.
significance: Sun stood still; Solomon's dream of wisdom

name: Gideon
description: A city, land, and valley near Zarahemla, east of the river Sidon. Named after the Nephite patriot Gideon.
significance: Where Alma preached important sermons about Christ's coming; location of Nehor's crime

name: Gilgal
description: First camp of Israel after crossing the Jordan. Twelve stones were set up here as a memorial. Saul was made king here. Samuel judged Israel here.
significance: Memorial of Jordan crossing; Saul's coronation site

name: Gomorrah
description: City destroyed alongside Sodom by divine judgment. Referenced throughout scripture as a warning against wickedness.
significance: Destroyed with Sodom as symbol of divine judgment

name: Goshen
description: Fertile region in the eastern Nile Delta where the Israelites lived during their sojourn in Egypt, from the time of Joseph until the Exodus.
significance: Israelite settlement in Egypt during the 400-year sojourn

name: Haran
description: City in upper Mesopotamia where Abraham's family settled after leaving Ur. Abraham departed here for Canaan. Jacob fled here to escape Esau and married Leah and Rachel.
significance: Abraham's departure point for Canaan; Jacob married here

name: Harmony
description: A town in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania where Joseph Smith translated much of the Book of Mormon and received priesthood keys.
significance: Where Joseph translated the Book of Mormon; where the Aaronic Priesthood was restored on the Susquehanna River

name: Helam
description: A land and city where Alma the Elder and his followers settled after fleeing from King Noah. They were later placed in bondage by the Lamanites.
significance: Where Alma's people established a righteous community and were miraculously delivered from bondage

name: Irreantum
description: The sea beheld by Lehi's family upon reaching the land of Bountiful, interpreted as meaning 'many waters.'
significance: The sea from which Nephi's family launched their voyage to the promised land

name: Ishmael
description: A Lamanite land called after the sons of Ishmael, where King Lamoni ruled and Ammon served as a servant.
significance: Where Ammon's famous mission took place, including defending the king's flocks at the waters of Sebus

name: Jackson County
description: A county in western Missouri identified as the location of Zion and the New Jerusalem. The Saints were violently expelled in 1833.
significance: Designated land of Zion; Saints were driven out; land purchases commanded by revelation

name: Jacob's Well
description: Well near Shechem/Sychar where Jesus met the Samaritan woman and offered her 'living water,' revealing himself as the Messiah.
significance: Jesus offered 'living water' to the Samaritan woman

name: Jershon
description: A land given by the Nephites to the converted Lamanites (Anti-Nephi-Lehies/people of Ammon) as a place of refuge.
significance: Safe haven provided by the Nephites for the pacifist Anti-Nephi-Lehies

name: Jezreel Valley
description: Large fertile valley in northern Israel, site of numerous battles. Gideon defeated the Midianites here. Saul died on nearby Mount Gilboa.
significance: Major battlefield; Gideon's victory; Saul's death

name: Joppa
description: Ancient seaport where Jonah boarded a ship fleeing from God. Peter raised Tabitha from the dead and received his vision of clean and unclean animals here.
significance: Jonah's departure port; Peter's vision of the Gentiles

name: Kadesh-barnea
description: Oasis in the Negev desert where Israel camped and sent spies into Canaan. The people's refusal to enter led to 40 years of wandering. Moses struck the rock here.
significance: Spies sent to Canaan; Israel condemned to wander 40 years

name: Kolob
description: The great governing star (or planet) nearest to the throne of God, where one revolution equals one thousand years of earth time.
significance: Represents God's dwelling place and the governing order of the cosmos in Abraham's vision

name: Laodicea
description: Wealthy city in Asia Minor and one of the seven churches in Revelation, rebuked for being 'lukewarm.'
significance: One of seven churches; rebuked for lukewarmness

name: Lebanon
description: Mountainous region north of Israel famous for its cedar trees, used to build Solomon's Temple.
significance: Cedars of Lebanon used in Solomon's Temple

name: Lehi-Nephi
description: The city of Nephi as known during Zeniff's era, combining the names of Lehi and Nephi.
significance: Center of Zeniff's colony; given to the Nephites by Lamanite king Laman as part of a deceptive treaty

name: Lemuel
description: A valley near the river Laman and the Red Sea where Lehi's family first camped. Also a later Lamanite city.
significance: First named campsite of Lehi's family in the wilderness; where Lehi gave his sons counsel

name: Macedonia
description: Roman province in northern Greece. Paul received a vision calling him here. Churches at Philippi, Thessalonica, and Berea were in Macedonia.
significance: Paul's Macedonian call; first European churches

name: Manchester
description: A town in Ontario County (now Wayne County), New York where the Smith family lived and where Joseph Smith had the First Vision and retrieved the gold plates.
significance: Site of the Sacred Grove (First Vision); near the Hill Cumorah where the plates were retrieved

name: Manti
description: A significant Nephite city in the southern part of Nephite territory, near the headwaters of the river Sidon.
significance: Key strategic military location during the wars with the Lamanites; site of Captain Moroni's campaigns

name: Mediterranean Sea
description: The large sea forming the western boundary of the Promised Land.
significance: Western boundary of the Promised Land

name: Megiddo
description: Strategic fortified city overlooking the Jezreel Valley. Solomon built it as a chariot city. King Josiah was killed here. Associated with the apocalyptic battle of Armageddon.
significance: Strategic fortress; associated with the battle of Armageddon

name: Midian
description: Region in northwestern Arabia where Moses fled after killing the Egyptian. He married Zipporah and encountered the burning bush here.
significance: Moses's refuge; burning bush encounter

name: Missouri
description: The state designated by revelation as the land of Zion and the inheritance of the Saints.
significance: Land of the Saints' inheritance; designated location of the New Jerusalem

name: Moab
description: Region east of the Dead Sea. Ruth was from Moab. Balaam was summoned to curse Israel from here. Moses viewed the Promised Land and died here.
significance: Ruth's homeland; Moses's death; Balaam's oracle

name: Moron
description: The land where the Jaredite kings dwelt and ruled, near the land called Desolation by the Nephites.
significance: Capital region of the Jaredite kingdom; where the throne was located

name: Moroni
description: A Nephite coastal city by the east sea, on the southern border of Nephite territory. It sank into the sea at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Strategic coastal city founded by Captain Moroni; destroyed by sinking into the sea at Christ's death

name: Mount Ararat
description: Mountain range where Noah's ark came to rest after the Flood receded. Located in the region of ancient Armenia.
significance: Resting place of Noah's ark after the Flood

name: Mount Hermon
description: Highest mountain in the region at the northern boundary of Israel. Its melting snow feeds the Jordan River. Possible site of the Transfiguration.
significance: Northern boundary of Israel; possible Transfiguration site

name: Mount Nebo
description: Mountain in Moab where Moses viewed the Promised Land before his death. God buried Moses in a valley in the land of Moab nearby.
significance: Where Moses saw the Promised Land before his death

name: Mount Tabor
description: Mountain in the Jezreel Valley. Deborah and Barak gathered forces here before defeating Sisera. Traditional site of the Transfiguration of Jesus.
significance: Deborah's victory; traditional Transfiguration site

name: Mount Zion
description: Originally the Jebusite fortress captured by David, later applied to the Temple Mount and all of Jerusalem. Frequently used as a symbol for God's holy dwelling.
significance: Symbol of God's dwelling; David's captured fortress

name: Mulek
description: A Nephite city on the east coast that was captured by the Lamanites and later retaken by Moroni and Teancum through a clever stratagem.
significance: Site of a major military campaign where Moroni and Teancum recaptured the city from the Lamanites

name: Nahom
description: A place in the Arabian wilderness where Ishmael was buried. The family turned eastward from here toward the coast.
significance: Burial place of Ishmael; notable as a pre-existing place name (not named by Lehi's group)

name: Nephihah
description: A Nephite city between the cities of Moroni and Aaron, and site of nearby plains. It was a significant location during the Lamanite wars.
significance: Important military location during Captain Moroni's campaigns against the Lamanites

name: Palmyra
description: A town in Wayne County, New York where the Smith family lived and where the Book of Mormon was first published.
significance: Smith family home; where the Book of Mormon was published at E.B. Grandin's print shop

name: Patmos
description: Small Greek island in the Aegean Sea where the apostle John was exiled and received the visions recorded in the book of Revelation.
significance: Where John received the Revelation

name: Peniel
description: Place east of the Jordan where Jacob wrestled with an angel through the night and was renamed Israel, meaning 'he who strives with God.'
significance: Jacob wrestled with God and was renamed Israel

name: Pergamum
description: City in Asia Minor and one of the seven churches addressed in Revelation. Called 'where Satan's throne is,' likely referring to the great altar of Zeus.
significance: One of seven churches in Revelation; 'where Satan's throne is'

name: Persia
description: Empire that conquered Babylon and allowed the Jews to return to Jerusalem under Cyrus. Setting of Esther, Ezra, and Nehemiah.
significance: Freed the Jews from Babylonian exile; setting of Esther

name: Philippi
description: Roman colony in Macedonia where Paul established the first European church. Lydia was baptized here. Paul and Silas were imprisoned and miraculously freed.
significance: First European church; Paul and Silas imprisoned here

name: Plain of Shinar
description: Plain in Mesopotamia where the Tower of Babel was built. Early post-flood civilization settled here. Later identified with Babylonia.
significance: Tower of Babel; origin of scattered languages

name: Samaria (Region)
description: Central region of Israel between Galilee and Judea, inhabited by the Samaritans in the NT period.
significance: Territory between Judea and Galilee; Samaritan homeland

name: Sebus
description: A place of water where Lamanite flocks were taken to drink and where robbers would scatter them. Site of Ammon's defense of king Lamoni's flocks.
significance: Where Ammon defended king Lamoni's flocks and cut off the arms of the attackers

name: Shechem
description: Central city in Canaan where Abraham first built an altar. Joseph's bones were buried here. Joshua made a covenant here. Jesus met the Samaritan woman near here.
significance: Abraham's first altar in Canaan; covenant renewal site

name: Shiloh
description: Central worship site where the Tabernacle stood for over 300 years during the period of the Judges. Samuel grew up serving here under Eli.
significance: First permanent home of the Tabernacle; Samuel's childhood home

name: Shim
description: A hill where Ammaron deposited the sacred records. Mormon later retrieved them from there.
significance: Hiding place of the sacred Nephite records before Mormon retrieved and abridged them

name: Sidon
description: Ancient Phoenician port city. Jezebel was a Sidonian princess. Elijah stayed with the widow of Zarephath near Sidon. Jesus visited the region.
significance: Major Phoenician city; home of Jezebel's family

name: Smyrna
description: City in Asia Minor and one of the seven churches in Revelation, praised for enduring tribulation and poverty.
significance: One of seven churches in Revelation; praised for faithfulness

name: Sodom
description: Wicked city near the Dead Sea destroyed by God with fire and brimstone. Lot and his family were rescued, but his wife looked back and became a pillar of salt.
significance: Symbol of divine judgment; destroyed for wickedness

name: Susa
description: Capital of the Persian Empire where Esther became queen, Haman plotted against the Jews, and Nehemiah served as cupbearer to King Artaxerxes.
significance: Setting of Esther; Nehemiah's starting point

name: Susquehanna
description: A river in Pennsylvania on whose banks the Aaronic and Melchizedek Priesthoods were restored and Michael detected the devil.
significance: Where John the Baptist and Peter, James, and John restored the priesthoods

name: Tarsus
description: Prominent city in Cilicia and the birthplace and hometown of the apostle Paul.
significance: Birthplace of the apostle Paul

name: Thessalonica
description: Major city in Macedonia where Paul established a church. He wrote two epistles to the Thessalonians addressing the Second Coming.
significance: Recipient of two Pauline epistles on the Second Coming

name: Tigris River
description: One of the four rivers of Eden and a major river of Mesopotamia. Daniel received a vision near the great river Hiddekel.
significance: River of Eden; Daniel's vision

name: Tyre
description: Major Phoenician port city. Hiram of Tyre supplied materials for Solomon's Temple. Ezekiel prophesied its destruction. Jesus visited the region.
significance: Supplied materials for the Temple; prophesied against by Ezekiel

name: Ur
description: Abraham's birthplace in Mesopotamia, from which God called him to journey to the Promised Land.
significance: Abraham's homeland; starting point of the covenant journey

name: Valley of Elah
description: Valley southwest of Jerusalem where the young David defeated Goliath the Philistine champion with a sling and stone.
significance: David defeated Goliath here

name: Valley of Hinnom
description: Valley south of Jerusalem where children were sacrificed to Molech. Jeremiah prophesied judgment on it. In NT times, used as a symbol for hell (Gehenna).
significance: Child sacrifice site; origin of the NT word for hell

name: Wilderness of Judea
description: Desert region east of Jerusalem descending toward the Dead Sea. David hid from Saul here. John the Baptist preached here. Jesus was tempted here for 40 days.
significance: David's refuge; Jesus's temptation; John the Baptist's ministry

name: Aaron
description: A Nephite city near the borders of Nephihah and Moroni. Also referenced as a place where prisoners fled during Mormon's era.
significance: Border city between Moroni and Nephihah in the eastern Nephite territory

name: Achaia
description: Roman province in southern Greece, with Corinth as its capital. Paul spent 18 months here.
significance: Province containing Corinth; Paul ministered 18 months

name: Adullam
description: City with a nearby cave where David hid from Saul and gathered 400 discontented followers who became his mighty men.
significance: David's hideout; gathered his mighty men

name: Ai
description: Canaanite city near Bethel. Israel was defeated here due to Achan's sin, then conquered it after dealing with the sin.
significance: Israel's initial defeat due to sin; then conquered

name: Alexandria
description: Major Egyptian port city. Apollos was from here. The Septuagint was produced here. Ships from Alexandria feature in Acts.
significance: Apollos's hometown; Septuagint origin; grain ships to Rome

name: Alma
description: A valley where Alma the Elder's people stopped during their exodus from the land of Helam and poured out thanks to God.
significance: Named after Alma; where the people gave thanks for their deliverance from Lamanite bondage

name: Ammon
description: Region east of the Jordan, homeland of the Ammonites. Their capital Rabbah was besieged by David's army when Uriah was killed.
significance: Ammonite homeland; Uriah killed during siege of Rabbah

name: Amulon
description: A land named after the former priest of King Noah who settled there with other priests and Lamanite women.
significance: Settlement of the wicked priests of Noah who became oppressors of Alma's people

name: Anathoth
description: Priestly city in Benjamin, hometown of the prophet Jeremiah.
significance: Hometown of Jeremiah

name: Antioch in Pisidia
description: City in Asia Minor where Paul preached a major sermon in the synagogue on his first missionary journey and turned to the Gentiles after Jewish rejection.
significance: Paul turned to the Gentiles after Jewish rejection

name: Antiparah
description: A Lamanite-held city in the southwestern theater. Helaman's warriors lured the Lamanite garrison out through a decoy march.
significance: Site of the famous decoy strategy that drew out Lamanite armies, leading to their defeat

name: Arabia
description: Vast peninsula south and east of Israel. Paul went to Arabia after his conversion before returning to Damascus.
significance: Paul went here after conversion; Sinai located here per Galatians

name: Arimathea
description: Hometown of Joseph of Arimathea, the wealthy member of the Sanhedrin who provided his own tomb for Jesus's burial.
significance: Home of Joseph who buried Jesus in his tomb

name: Arnon River
description: River gorge in Transjordan that served as the border between Moab and the Amorite territory conquered by Israel.
significance: Boundary between Moab and Israel's Transjordan territory

name: Ashdod
description: One of the five major Philistine cities. The Ark of the Covenant was brought here to the temple of Dagon, which fell before it. Philip preached here after baptizing the Ethiopian eunuch.
significance: Dagon's temple fell before the Ark here

name: Ashkelon
description: One of the five major Philistine cities on the Mediterranean coast. Samson killed thirty men here. Mentioned in prophetic oracles.
significance: Major Philistine city

name: Bashan
description: Fertile region east of the Sea of Galilee. Israel defeated King Og of Bashan before entering the Promised Land.
significance: Defeat of King Og; known for fertility and strength

name: Beer-lahai-roi
description: Well in the Negev between Kadesh and Bered where the angel of the Lord appeared to Hagar after she fled from Sarah. Name means 'Well of the Living One who sees me.'
significance: Hagar's encounter with the angel; God sees the outcast

name: Berea
description: Macedonian city where Paul preached after leaving Thessalonica. The Bereans were praised for examining the Scriptures daily to verify Paul's teaching.
significance: Noble Bereans searched scriptures daily

name: Beth-horon
description: Twin cities on the road from Jerusalem to the coast. Joshua's army chased the Amorites here while God hurled hailstones.
significance: God hurled hailstones on the Amorites here

name: Beth-shan
description: City where the Philistines displayed the bodies of Saul and his sons on the city wall after Gilboa.
significance: Saul's body displayed on its walls

name: Beth-shemesh
description: City in Judah where the Ark of the Covenant was returned by the Philistines on a cart pulled by cows.
significance: Ark of the Covenant returned here by the Philistines

name: Bethabara
description: A place beyond Jordan referenced in Nephi's vision where his father Lehi saw the Messiah would be baptized.
significance: Prophesied location of the Messiah's baptism by John

name: Bethphage
description: Village on the Mount of Olives from which Jesus sent two disciples to fetch the donkey for his triumphal entry into Jerusalem.
significance: Starting point of Jesus's triumphal entry

name: Bozrah
description: Capital of Edom, prophesied against by Isaiah, Jeremiah, and Amos.
significance: Edomite capital; Isaiah's judgment vision

name: Brook Cherith
description: Brook east of the Jordan where God commanded Elijah to hide during the drought. Ravens brought him bread and meat.
significance: Elijah fed by ravens during the drought

name: Carchemish
description: City on the Euphrates where Nebuchadnezzar defeated Pharaoh Necho in 605 BC, establishing Babylonian dominance.
significance: Nebuchadnezzar defeated Egypt; shifted world power

name: Chorazin
description: Town near the Sea of Galilee denounced by Jesus along with Bethsaida and Capernaum for not repenting despite witnessing many miracles.
significance: Denounced by Jesus for unrepentance

name: Colesville
description: A town in Broome County, New York where an early branch of the Church was established.
significance: Home of an early branch of the Church; Saints were persecuted and later gathered to Ohio and Missouri

name: Colossae
description: City in Asia Minor near Laodicea. Paul wrote his Epistle to the Colossians to the church here.
significance: Recipient of Paul's Epistle to the Colossians

name: Corihor
description: A Jaredite land and valley where armies gathered. The people of Shiz fled there during the final Jaredite wars.
significance: Major gathering place during the final Jaredite civil war between Coriantumr and Shiz

name: Crete
description: Large Mediterranean island. Paul sailed past it on his voyage to Rome. Titus was left here to appoint elders in every city.
significance: Titus appointed elders here; Paul's sea voyage

name: Cumeni
description: A Nephite city captured by the Lamanites and retaken by Helaman and his stripling warriors through siege.
significance: Retaken by Helaman's 2,000 stripling warriors during the southwest campaign

name: Cyrene
description: Greek city in North Africa. Simon of Cyrene carried Jesus's cross. People from Cyrene were present at Pentecost.
significance: Simon of Cyrene carried Jesus's cross

name: Decapolis
description: League of ten Greco-Roman cities mostly east of the Jordan. Jesus ministered here, including healing the Gadarene demoniac.
significance: Gentile region where Jesus ministered and healed

name: Dothan
description: City where Joseph's brothers sold him into slavery. Later, Elisha's servant saw the hills full of horses and chariots of fire here.
significance: Joseph sold into slavery; Elisha's angelic army vision

name: Ekron
description: Northernmost of the five major Philistine cities. The Ark was sent here before being returned to Israel. Baal-zebub, god of Ekron, was consulted by King Ahaziah.
significance: Philistine city; home of Baal-zebub

name: Elim
description: Oasis with twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees where Israel camped after crossing the Red Sea.
significance: Oasis of refreshment during the Exodus

name: En-gedi
description: Oasis on the western shore of the Dead Sea where David hid from Saul in caves and spared Saul's life.
significance: David hid from Saul; spared Saul's life in a cave

name: Endor
description: Town where Saul consulted a medium the night before the battle of Gilboa, and the spirit of Samuel appeared to him.
significance: Saul consulted the medium; Samuel's spirit appeared

name: Ezion-geber
description: Port city at the head of the Gulf of Aqaba from which Solomon launched ships to Ophir for gold trade.
significance: Solomon's port for the Ophir gold trade

name: Gadara
description: City east of the Sea of Galilee where Jesus cast a legion of demons out of a man, sending them into a herd of swine.
significance: Jesus cast out Legion; swine ran into the sea

name: Gezer
description: Strategic Canaanite city. Pharaoh conquered it and gave it to Solomon as a dowry. Solomon fortified it along with Hazor and Megiddo.
significance: Solomon's fortified city; Pharaoh's dowry gift

name: Gibeah
description: City in Benjamin and King Saul's hometown and capital. Scene of the horrific crime that led to civil war against Benjamin.
significance: Saul's capital; civil war trigger

name: Gid
description: A Nephite city on the eastern borders by the seashore, captured by the Lamanites and later recaptured.
significance: One of the east coast cities captured during the Lamanite invasion

name: Gihon Spring
description: Jerusalem's main water source. Solomon was anointed king here. Hezekiah built his tunnel from here to the Pool of Siloam.
significance: Solomon's anointing; Hezekiah's tunnel

name: Gilead
description: Mountainous region east of the Jordan, known for its balm. Jacob and Laban made a covenant here. Elijah was from Gilead.
significance: Elijah's homeland; famous for its healing balm

name: Gilgal
description: A Nephite city that was sunk into the earth at Christ's death. Also a valley in the land of the Jaredites where Coriantumr and Shared battled.
significance: Destroyed at Christ's death; also a major Jaredite battle site

name: Hamath
description: Syrian city on the Orontes River marking the northern boundary of the Promised Land.
significance: Northern boundary of the Promised Land

name: Hazor
description: Largest Canaanite city in the north, conquered by Joshua. Its king Jabin oppressed Israel before Deborah and Barak defeated him. Solomon later fortified it.
significance: Largest Canaanite city; conquered by Joshua, fortified by Solomon

name: Hermounts
description: A wilderness region west and north of Zarahemla, infested with wild and ravenous beasts.
significance: Where the Amlicites and Lamanites fled after being driven out by the Nephites

name: Heshbon
description: Capital of Sihon, king of the Amorites, defeated by Israel before crossing the Jordan.
significance: Sihon's capital; defeated before entering Canaan

name: Iconium
description: City in Asia Minor where Paul and Barnabas preached on their first missionary journey, winning many converts but facing opposition.
significance: Paul's first missionary journey; mixed reception

name: Idumea
description: Region south of Judea, homeland of Esau's descendants. The Herods were of Idumean descent.
significance: Esau's descendants; Herod's family origin

name: Jabbok River
description: River east of the Jordan that Jacob crossed the night he wrestled with the angel at Peniel.
significance: Jacob wrestled with the angel at its ford

name: Jabesh-gilead
description: City east of the Jordan whose men rescued the bodies of Saul and his sons from the Philistines.
significance: Saul rescued it; its men honored Saul in death

name: Jershon (Abraham)
description: A place on the route from Haran to Canaan where Abraham built an altar and prayed for his father's household.
significance: Where Abraham built an altar and offered sacrifice on the way to Canaan

name: Jerusalem (BoM)
description: A city in the land of Nephi, near the waters of Mormon, distinct from the Old World Jerusalem. Destroyed by water at Christ's death.
significance: A New World city that was destroyed by flooding at the time of Christ's crucifixion

name: Jezreel
description: City in the valley of the same name. Ahab had a palace here. Naboth's vineyard was here, which Jezebel seized. Jehu killed Joram and Jezebel here.
significance: Ahab's palace; Naboth's vineyard; Jezebel's death

name: Kidron Valley
description: Valley between Jerusalem and the Mount of Olives. David crossed it fleeing Absalom. Jesus crossed it going to Gethsemane. Idols were burned here by righteous kings.
significance: David fled Absalom; Jesus crossed to Gethsemane

name: Kiriath-jearim
description: City where the Ark of the Covenant was kept for 20 years in the house of Abinadab, until David brought it to Jerusalem.
significance: Ark stored here for 20 years before David moved it

name: Lachish
description: Major fortified city in Judah conquered by Joshua. Sennacherib besieged it during his campaign against Judah. Nebuchadnezzar destroyed it before Jerusalem fell.
significance: Key Judean fortress; besieged by Sennacherib and Nebuchadnezzar

name: Laman
description: A river named by Lehi that emptied into the Red Sea, located at the family's first wilderness camp.
significance: Named by Lehi as a symbol, wishing his son Laman would be like the river continually running into righteousness

name: Lehi
description: A Nephite city on the eastern seaboard that was captured by the Lamanites during the wars recorded in Alma.
significance: One of several east coast cities captured and later recovered during the Nephite-Lamanite wars

name: Lydda
description: City in the coastal plain where Peter healed Aeneas, a man who had been paralyzed for eight years.
significance: Peter healed Aeneas of paralysis

name: Lystra
description: City in Asia Minor where Paul healed a lame man and the people tried to worship him as Hermes. Paul was later stoned here. Timothy's hometown.
significance: Paul stoned; Timothy's hometown

name: Magdala
description: Town on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee, hometown of Mary Magdalene.
significance: Hometown of Mary Magdalene

name: Malta
description: Mediterranean island where Paul was shipwrecked on his voyage to Rome. A viper bit him but he was unharmed. He healed the father of Publius here.
significance: Paul's shipwreck; healing miracles

name: Marah
description: Place in the wilderness where the Israelites found bitter water. God showed Moses a tree to make the water sweet.
significance: Bitter water made sweet; God tested Israel

name: Melek
description: A Nephite land west of the river Sidon where Alma preached with great success.
significance: Location of Alma's successful missionary work before traveling to Ammonihah

name: Memphis
description: Ancient Egyptian capital on the Nile. Isaiah, Jeremiah, and Ezekiel prophesied against it.
significance: Egyptian capital; prophesied against by major prophets

name: Michmash
description: Town where Jonathan and his armor-bearer climbed a cliff to attack the Philistine garrison, triggering a rout.
significance: Jonathan's bold attack on the Philistine garrison

name: Middoni
description: A Lamanite land ruled by king Antiomno, where Aaron, Muloki, and Ammah were imprisoned.
significance: Where Ammon and Lamoni traveled to free imprisoned missionaries

name: Miletus
description: Port city in Asia Minor where Paul gave his farewell address to the Ephesian elders on his way to Jerusalem.
significance: Paul's farewell to the Ephesian elders

name: Mizpah
description: Gathering place for Israel's assemblies during the Judges and Samuel. Saul was selected king here. Gedaliah governed from here after Jerusalem fell.
significance: National assembly site; Saul chosen king; post-exile governor

name: Moreh
description: Plains in Canaan near Sechem where Abraham offered sacrifice and received the promise of the land.
significance: Where God appeared to Abraham and promised the land to his seed

name: Morianton
description: A Nephite city and land near the east seashore. Its people attempted to flee northward but were stopped by Moroni's forces.
significance: Site of a border dispute that nearly led to Lamanite access to the land northward

name: Moronihah
description: A great Nephite city that was covered with earth at the time of Christ's crucifixion, with a great mountain forming in its place.
significance: Buried by earthquake at Christ's death as divine judgment for the wickedness of its inhabitants

name: Mount Ebal
description: Mountain north of Shechem where Joshua built an altar and the curses of the Law were pronounced, as Moses had commanded.
significance: Site of covenant curses ceremony under Joshua

name: Mount Gerizim
description: Mountain south of Shechem where the blessings of the Law were pronounced. Later became the sacred mountain of the Samaritans.
significance: Blessings mountain; Samaritan worship center

name: Mount Gilboa
description: Mountain where King Saul and his sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchishua died in battle against the Philistines. David lamented their deaths.
significance: Death of Saul and Jonathan

name: Mount Hor
description: Mountain on the border of Edom where Aaron died and was buried. His priestly garments were transferred to his son Eleazar.
significance: Aaron's death and burial; priestly succession

name: Mount Seir
description: Mountain range in Edom, homeland of Esau and his descendants. Prophets pronounced judgment on it.
significance: Homeland of Esau/Edom

name: Nain
description: Small village in Galilee where Jesus raised a widow's only son from the dead as the funeral procession was leaving the city.
significance: Jesus raised a widow's son from the dead

name: Negev
description: Arid region in southern Israel where the patriarchs pastured their flocks.
significance: Patriarchal grazing lands; southern boundary region

name: New York
description: The city where the bishop was commanded to go and warn the people with the sound of the gospel.
significance: One of three major cities where the bishop was commanded to preach

name: Nimrod
description: A valley named after the mighty hunter where the Lord spoke to the brother of Jared before the Jaredite departure.
significance: Where the Lord gave instructions to the brother of Jared for the journey to the promised land

name: Noah
description: A previously weak Nephite city that Captain Moroni fortified to exceed the strength of Ammonihah.
significance: Demonstrated Moroni's strategic military fortification tactics

name: Nob
description: City of priests where David received the showbread and Goliath's sword from Ahimelech. Saul later massacred 85 priests here.
significance: David received showbread; Saul massacred the priests

name: Nod
description: A land east of Eden where Cain dwelt with his wife and brethren after being shut out from the Lord's presence.
significance: Where Cain settled after being cursed; beginning of a wicked lineage

name: Olaha Shinehah
description: A place described as the land where Adam dwelt, mentioned alongside the mountains of Adam-ondi-Ahman.
significance: Described as the land where Adam dwelt; associated with the primeval history of the earth

name: Olishem
description: A plain near Potiphar's Hill where the altar of the priest of Pharaoh stood.
significance: Location of the altar where Abraham was nearly sacrificed to heathen gods

name: Omner
description: A Nephite city on the eastern borders by the seashore, captured by the Lamanites.
significance: One of the east coast cities captured by Amalickiah's forces

name: Onidah
description: A place and hill where Lamanites fled for safety and where Alma preached to the poor Zoramites.
significance: Where Alma delivered his famous sermon on faith to the humble Zoramites

name: Ophir
description: Famed land rich in gold, to which Solomon sent ships from Ezion-geber. Its exact location is unknown.
significance: Source of Solomon's gold

name: Ophrah
description: Hometown of Gideon in Manasseh. The angel of the Lord appeared to Gideon here, calling him to deliver Israel from Midian.
significance: Gideon's hometown; angel's call under the oak

name: Philadelphia
description: City in Asia Minor and one of the seven churches in Revelation, praised and given an 'open door' with no rebuke from Christ.
significance: One of seven churches; praised with no rebuke

name: Pithom
description: One of the two store cities built by enslaved Israelites for Pharaoh, along with Raamses, before the Exodus.
significance: Built by Israelite slave labor

name: Pool of Bethesda
description: Pool in Jerusalem with five porticoes where Jesus healed a man who had been an invalid for 38 years.
significance: Jesus healed the 38-year invalid

name: Pool of Siloam
description: Pool in Jerusalem fed by Hezekiah's tunnel where Jesus sent the blind man to wash and receive his sight.
significance: Blind man healed; Hezekiah's tunnel

name: Potiphar's Hill
description: A hill in the land of Ur of Chaldea where the priest of Pharaoh offered sacrifices, including a child offering, upon an altar.
significance: Site of the altar where Abraham was nearly sacrificed and where human sacrifices were offered

name: Raamses
description: Store city built by Israelite slaves and their departure point for the Exodus.
significance: Departure point of the Exodus; built by Israelite slaves

name: Ramah
description: City in Benjamin, Samuel's hometown and the place where he judged Israel. Rachel's weeping was associated with Ramah.
significance: Samuel's hometown; Rachel's weeping

name: Ramoth-gilead
description: City of refuge east of the Jordan. Ahab was killed in battle here. Jehu was anointed king here.
significance: City of refuge; Ahab killed; Jehu anointed king

name: Rephidim
description: Place where Israel fought the Amalekites while Moses held up his hands. Also where Moses struck the rock at Massah and Meribah for water.
significance: Battle with Amalek; Moses struck the rock

name: Riplah
description: A hill near the river Sidon where Captain Moroni concealed part of his army to ambush the Lamanites led by Zerahemnah.
significance: Site of Moroni's strategic ambush leading to the defeat of Zerahemnah

name: Ripliancum
description: Large waters in the land northward, interpreted as meaning 'large, or to exceed all.' Site of a major Jaredite battle.
significance: Near-final battle site in the destruction of the Jaredite civilization

name: Sardis
description: City in Asia Minor and one of the seven churches in Revelation, warned that it was spiritually dead and needed to wake up.
significance: One of seven churches; warned of spiritual death

name: Sechem
description: A place in the plains of Moreh in Canaan where Abraham offered sacrifice after arriving in the promised land.
significance: Where God promised Abraham the land of Canaan for his seed

name: Sela
description: Edomite city, likely the famous rock-cut city of Petra. Amaziah captured it.
significance: Edomite fortress captured by Amaziah; likely Petra

name: Sharon
description: Fertile coastal plain along the Mediterranean, famous for its beauty. 'Rose of Sharon' appears in Song of Solomon.
significance: Symbol of beauty; 'Rose of Sharon'

name: Sharon
description: A town in Windsor County, Vermont where Joseph Smith was born on December 23, 1805.
significance: Birthplace of the Prophet Joseph Smith

name: Shazer
description: A place in the wilderness where Lehi's family camped during their journey through Arabia, used as a base for hunting.
significance: Named campsite during Lehi's wilderness journey near the Red Sea

name: Sheba
description: Kingdom in southern Arabia. The Queen of Sheba visited Solomon to test his wisdom and was overwhelmed by his wealth and knowledge.
significance: Queen of Sheba visited Solomon

name: Shem
description: A Nephite city that Mormon fortified and gathered his people into during the final wars with the Lamanites.
significance: Where Mormon rallied the Nephites and temporarily stopped the Lamanite advance

name: Shemlon
description: A land possessed by the Lamanites, near the lands of Shilom and Nephi. Zeniff sent spies to observe Lamanite preparations there.
significance: Lamanite territory from which attacks on Zeniff's people were launched

name: Shilom
description: A city and land near the city of Nephi (Lehi-Nephi), part of the territory given by king Laman to Zeniff's people.
significance: Part of the Zeniffite settlement in the land of Nephi; site of military lookout tower

name: Shunem
description: Town in the Jezreel Valley where a wealthy woman hosted Elisha. When her son died, Elisha raised him from the dead.
significance: Elisha raised the Shunammite's son from the dead

name: Sidom
description: A land where the believers who were cast out of Ammonihah gathered. Alma and Amulek ministered to them there.
significance: Place of refuge for those expelled from Ammonihah for believing in Alma's words

name: Simeon
description: A mountain where the Lord commanded Enoch to go. From its top, Enoch beheld the heavens open and was clothed in glory.
significance: Where Enoch had his great vision, seeing the Lord face to face

name: Tarshish
description: Distant maritime destination, possibly in Spain. Jonah tried to flee here instead of going to Nineveh. Solomon's fleet traded there.
significance: Jonah's attempted escape destination; symbol of distant lands

name: Teancum
description: A Nephite city in the borders by the seashore, near the city Desolation. Its inhabitants were driven out by the Lamanites.
significance: Coastal city near Desolation; its inhabitants were taken prisoner and sacrificed to idol gods

name: Tekoa
description: Town south of Bethlehem, hometown of the prophet Amos. A wise woman from Tekoa persuaded David to reconcile with Absalom.
significance: Hometown of Amos

name: Thyatira
description: City in Asia Minor and one of the seven churches in Revelation. Home of Lydia, the seller of purple cloth whom Paul baptized in Philippi.
significance: One of seven churches; home of Lydia

name: Tiberias
description: City on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee built by Herod Antipas. The sea was sometimes called the Sea of Tiberias.
significance: Gave alternate name to the Sea of Galilee

name: Tirzah
description: First capital of the northern kingdom of Israel before Omri moved it to Samaria. Known for its beauty.
significance: First capital of the northern kingdom

name: Troas
description: Port city in northwest Asia Minor where Paul received the Macedonian vision calling him to Europe. Eutychus fell from a window here during Paul's sermon.
significance: Paul's Macedonian call vision; Eutychus raised

name: Valley of Achor
description: Valley near Jericho where Achan was executed for taking forbidden plunder. Hosea promised it would become a 'door of hope.'
significance: Achan's sin punished; Hosea's 'door of hope'

name: Valley of Aijalon
description: Valley where the moon stood still at Joshua's command during the battle against the Amorite coalition.
significance: Moon stood still at Joshua's command

name: Wilderness of Paran
description: Large desert region in the northeastern Sinai Peninsula where Ishmael grew up and where the Israelites wandered. The spies were sent from here.
significance: Ishmael's home; departure point for the spies

name: Wilderness of Shur
description: Desert region east of Egypt that the Israelites entered immediately after crossing the Red Sea.
significance: First wilderness after the Red Sea crossing

name: Wilderness of Sin
description: Desert region between Elim and Sinai where God first provided manna and quail to feed the Israelites during the Exodus.
significance: God first provided manna to Israel

name: Wilderness of Zin
description: Desert region in the Negev where Kadesh-barnea was located. Miriam died and was buried here. Moses struck the rock for water.
significance: Miriam's burial; Moses struck the rock

name: Zarephath
description: Phoenician town between Tyre and Sidon where Elijah stayed with a widow whose flour and oil did not run out, and whose son he raised from the dead.
significance: Widow's miraculous provision; her son raised by Elijah

name: Ziklag
description: Philistine city given to David by Achish while David was fleeing from Saul. Amalekites raided it but David recovered everything.
significance: David's refuge from Saul; raided by Amalekites

name: Zoar
description: Small city near Sodom to which Lot fled when Sodom was destroyed. God spared it at Lot's request.
significance: Lot's refuge from Sodom's destruction

name: Zorah
description: Town in the territory of Dan, birthplace of Samson.
significance: Birthplace of Samson

name: Ablom
description: A place by the seashore where king Omer fled with his family to escape the conspiracy of his son Jared.
significance: Refuge of king Omer after being warned by God to flee

name: Agosh
description: Plains in Jaredite territory where Coriantumr fought and killed Lib during the final wars.
significance: Battle site where Lib was killed and his brother Shiz took command

name: Akish
description: A wilderness area in Jaredite territory where multiple battles took place between Coriantumr and his enemies.
significance: Repeated battle site during the final Jaredite wars

name: Amnihu
description: A hill east of the river Sidon, near Zarahemla, where the Amlicites gathered to begin their war against the Nephites.
significance: Starting point of the Amlicite rebellion against the Nephite government

name: Angola
description: A city where Mormon's army took temporary refuge during the final Nephite retreat before being driven out by the Lamanites.
significance: One of the cities Mormon attempted to defend during the final Nephite retreat northward

name: Ani-Anti
description: A village where Muloki and Ammah preached the word of God to the Lamanites.
significance: One of the mission locations of the sons of Mosiah among the Lamanites

name: Antum
description: A land containing the hill Shim where Ammaron deposited the sacred records for Mormon to retrieve.
significance: Location of the hill Shim and the deposit of sacred Nephite records

name: Boaz
description: A city where the Nephites briefly stood against the Lamanites during their final retreat before being driven out.
significance: One of the last cities defended by the Nephites before the final retreat to Cumorah

name: Cainan
description: A land of promise named after Cainan, son of Enos. Enoch later came out from this land before his prophetic ministry.
significance: Enoch's homeland; a land of righteousness from which he began his prophetic mission

name: Cenchreae
description: Eastern port of Corinth where Paul shaved his head in fulfillment of a vow. Phoebe served the church here.
significance: Paul's vow; Phoebe's church

name: Comnor
description: A Jaredite hill near the valley of Shurr where Coriantumr gathered his armies and challenged Shiz to battle.
significance: Site of a major battle between Coriantumr and Shiz's armies

name: David
description: A land from which the Nephites were driven during their final retreat northward.
significance: Part of the Nephite retreat route in the final wars

name: Derbe
description: City in Asia Minor and the farthest point of Paul's first missionary journey.
significance: Farthest point of Paul's first journey

name: Enoch (land)
description: One of several lands seen by Enoch in his vision, listed alongside Sharon, Omner, Heni, Shem, Haner, and Hanannihah.
significance: Part of Enoch's prophetic commission

name: Fair Havens
description: Harbor on the southern coast of Crete where Paul's ship stopped. Paul warned against continuing the voyage.
significance: Paul warned of danger if they sailed on

name: Gad
description: A city burned with fire at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed by fire at Christ's death

name: Gadiandi
description: A city that was sunk and replaced by hills and valleys at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed at Christ's death; inhabitants buried in the earth

name: Gadiomnah
description: A city that was sunk and replaced by hills and valleys at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed at Christ's death; inhabitants buried in the earth

name: Gimgimno
description: A city that was sunk and replaced by hills and valleys at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed at Christ's death

name: Hai
description: A place east of Bethel where Abraham pitched his tent in Canaan.
significance: Geographical marker for Abraham's camp in Canaan, east of Bethel

name: Hanannihah
description: One of several lands seen by Enoch in his vision, listed alongside Sharon, Enoch, Omner, Heni, Shem, and Haner.
significance: Part of Enoch's prophetic commission

name: Haner
description: One of several lands seen by Enoch in his vision, listed alongside Sharon, Enoch, Omner, Heni, Shem, and Hanannihah.
significance: Part of Enoch's prophetic commission

name: Heni
description: One of several lands seen by Enoch in his vision, listed alongside Sharon, Enoch, Omner, Shem, Haner, and Hanannihah.
significance: Part of Enoch's prophetic commission

name: Heshlon
description: Plains in Jaredite territory where Coriantumr pursued Shared after defeating him in the valley of Gilgal.
significance: Battle site in the Jaredite civil war between Coriantumr and Shared

name: Heth
description: A Jaredite land where Jared rebelled against his father and gained half the kingdom.
significance: Site of a rebellion that split the Jaredite kingdom

name: Jacob
description: A city that was sunk and replaced by hills and valleys at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed at Christ's death

name: Jacobugath
description: A great city inhabited by the people of king Jacob, burned with fire at Christ's crucifixion because of secret combinations.
significance: Destroyed by fire for extreme wickedness and secret combinations

name: Jashon
description: A city and land near the hill Shim where the Nephites retreated. Mormon retrieved the plates of Nephi from nearby.
significance: Near the hill Shim where the sacred records were deposited

name: Jordan
description: A Nephite city mentioned in the context of the destructions at Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Named in the account of cities destroyed at Christ's death

name: Josh
description: A city burned with fire at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed by fire at Christ's death for wickedness

name: Joshua
description: A land in the borders west by the seashore where Mormon's retreating Nephite army gathered.
significance: Temporary staging ground during the Nephite retreat

name: Kishkumen
description: A city burned with fire at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed by fire at Christ's death for wickedness

name: Laman (city)
description: A city burned with fire at the time of Christ's crucifixion because of wickedness.
significance: Destroyed by fire at Christ's death for casting out prophets

name: Lemuel (city)
description: A city in the land of Nephi where Amulon was appointed teacher over the Lamanite people.
significance: Lamanite city under the teaching influence of Amulon and the priests of Noah

name: Mahujah
description: A place where Enoch stood and cried unto the Lord, receiving a voice from heaven telling him to go to mount Simeon.
significance: Where Enoch received his prophetic calling from the Lord

name: Minon
description: A land above the land of Zarahemla, in the course of the land of Nephi, where a large Lamanite army was spotted.
significance: Where Nephite scouts discovered the Lamanite-Amlicite alliance marching against Zarahemla

name: Mocum
description: A city destroyed by being covered with water at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed by flooding at Christ's death

name: Moriantum
description: A place referenced by Mormon where Nephite soldiers committed atrocities against captured Lamanite women.
significance: Referenced in Mormon's lament over the moral degradation of both Nephites and Lamanites

name: Myra
description: City in Lycia where Paul's centurion transferred him to an Alexandrian grain ship sailing for Italy.
significance: Paul changed ships on voyage to Rome

name: Neapolis
description: Port city in Macedonia where Paul first set foot in Europe on his second missionary journey.
significance: Paul's first European landing point

name: Noah (waters)
description: Waters near the land of Nephi mentioned in the context of Lamanite-Nephite conflicts.
significance: Site of a battle between Zeniff's people and the Lamanites

name: Ogath
description: A place where Coriantumr's armies pitched their tents during the final Jaredite wars after fleeing southward.
significance: Campsite during the final Jaredite conflict near the hill Ramah/Cumorah

name: Omner (Moses)
description: One of several lands seen by Enoch in his vision, listed alongside Sharon, Enoch, Heni, Shem, Haner, and Hanannihah.
significance: Part of Enoch's prophetic commission

name: On
description: Ancient Egyptian city of the sun. Joseph married Asenath, daughter of Potiphera, priest of On.
significance: Joseph married into the priestly family here

name: Onihah
description: A city destroyed by being covered with water at the time of Christ's crucifixion.
significance: Destroyed by flooding at Christ's death

name: Paphos
description: City on Cyprus where Paul confronted the sorcerer Bar-Jesus and converted the proconsul Sergius Paulus.
significance: Paul confronted Bar-Jesus; proconsul converted

name: Perga
description: City in Pamphylia where John Mark left Paul and Barnabas during the first missionary journey.
significance: John Mark deserted the mission here

name: Pool of Gibeon
description: Large pool at Gibeon where Joab's men and Abner's men fought in a deadly contest during the civil war between David and Ish-bosheth.
significance: Joab vs Abner's deadly contest during civil war

name: Ptolemais
description: Port city on the Mediterranean coast. Paul stopped here briefly on his return from his third missionary journey.
significance: Paul visited briefly; ancient Phoenician port

name: Puteoli
description: Port city near Naples where Paul landed on his voyage to Rome and found Christian brothers.
significance: Paul's landing port in Italy on his way to Rome

name: Rhodes
description: Greek island that Paul's ship passed on his return from his third missionary journey.
significance: Paul passed through on his third journey

name: Salamis
description: Port city on Cyprus where Paul and Barnabas first preached at the start of their first missionary journey.
significance: First stop on Paul's first missionary journey

name: Samothrace
description: Island in the northern Aegean where Paul stopped on his voyage from Troas to Neapolis.
significance: Waypoint on Paul's journey to Europe

name: Seleucia
description: Port city of Antioch in Syria from which Paul and Barnabas sailed to Cyprus to begin their first missionary journey.
significance: Departure port for Paul's first missionary journey

name: Sharon (Moses)
description: A land seen by Enoch in his vision, listed alongside the lands of Enoch, Omner, Heni, Shem, Haner, and Hanannihah.
significance: One of several lands whose inhabitants Enoch was commanded to call to repentance

name: Shem (Moses)
description: One of several lands seen by Enoch in his vision, listed alongside Sharon, Enoch, Omner, Heni, Haner, and Hanannihah.
significance: Part of Enoch's prophetic commission

name: Sherrizah
description: A place with a tower where the Lamanites held many Nephite prisoners, including women and children, during the final wars.
significance: Site of atrocities against Nephite prisoners described in Moroni's letters from Mormon

name: Shimnilom
description: A Lamanite city mentioned alongside Shilom, Shemlon, and Lemuel in the land of Nephi.
significance: One of several cities where Amulon was appointed teacher over the Lamanites

name: Shulon
description: A land from which Enos and the people of God departed to dwell in a land of promise named Cainan.
significance: Early dwelling place of the people of God before their migration

name: Shum
description: A valley where Enoch beheld a great people dwelling in tents, called the people of Shum.
significance: Part of Enoch's prophetic vision of the peoples of the earth

name: Shurr
description: A Jaredite valley near the hill Comnor where Coriantumr pitched his tents during the final wars.
significance: Staging ground for the battle at the hill Comnor

name: Siron
description: A land among the borders of the Lamanites where the harlot Isabel lived. Alma's son Corianton went there.
significance: Where Corianton abandoned his mission to consort with the harlot Isabel

name: Syracuse
description: Major city on Sicily where Paul's ship stopped for three days on his voyage from Malta to Rome.
significance: Paul stopped here en route to Rome

name: Thompson
description: A township in Geauga County, Ohio where early Saints settled but experienced rebellion and stiffneckedness.
significance: Early gathering place where some Saints refused to live the law of consecration

name: Zeezrom
description: A Nephite city named after the lawyer Zeezrom, located in the southwestern region near Manti.
significance: One of the cities held by the Lamanites in the southwest war zone
